prevalence.source: European Thyroid Association (ETA) context; ultrasound epidemiology
prevalence.trend: increasing detection due to imaging use

burden.dalyPerCapita: low; burden driven by work-up and overdiagnosis

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: TSH and thyroid ultrasound; risk stratification by ultrasound features; FNA for suspicious nodules (Bethesda)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no routine screening; incidental detection common
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High; structured surveillance reduces unnecessary surgery

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: ETA-aligned ultrasound risk stratification and FNA when indicated
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Observation for low-risk nodules; FNA and surgery for suspicious/malignant nodules
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Overdiagnosis and variable FNA thresholds across countries
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Broad access to ultrasound and endocrinology services
